channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Four US military personnel die in non-combat plane crash in western Iraq
item.description: Four of the six crew members aboard a US military refuelling aircraft, the KC-135, were confirmed dead following a crash in western Iraq on Thursday. The US Central Command stated that the incident wa...

item.title: NU women's tennis team advances to UAAP finals
item.description: The National University (NU) women's tennis team has officially secured their place in the finals of the UAAP Season 88 tennis tournament. Demonstrating consistency and strong performance throughout t...

item.title: How Sudden Climate Events Shape Financial Markets and Pensions
item.description: This article explores how sudden climate shocks, such as floods or extreme storms, can rapidly disrupt financial systems and pension investments compared to slower environmental changes. Using the flo...

item.title: TPDC directed to complete ongoing oil and gas projects efficiently
item.description: The Permanent Secretary for Oil and Gas in Tanzania, James Mataragio, has instructed the Tanzania Petroleum Development Corporation (TPDC) to ensure that all ongoing oil and gas projects are completed...

item.title: Australian Rhodamnia zombi Tree Faces Extinction from Myrtle Rust
item.description: A newly identified Australian tree species, Rhodamnia zombi, has been nicknamed the “zombie tree” because it remains alive but is unable to reproduce due to the fungal disease myrtle rust. Since its f...

item.title: Parks Canada proposes controlled deer reduction on Grosse-Île to protect safety and rare plants
item.description: Parks Canada has announced plans to reduce the white-tailed deer population on Grosse-Île, a historic island in the St. Lawrence River near Quebec City. Officials say the current deer population, esti...

item.title: Shahrnush Parsipur’s Women Without Men: A Literary Act of Feminist Resistance in Iran
item.description: Shahrnush Parsipur’s novel 'Women Without Men' (Zanan bedun-e Mardan) stands as a bold literary work confronting Iranian patriarchy. Written in 1989, the book was quickly banned, and Parsipur faced im...

item.title: Ontario court permits Al-Quds Day rally in Toronto despite provincial injunction request
item.description: An Ontario Superior Court justice has ruled that the Al-Quds Day rally in Toronto can proceed as scheduled on Saturday afternoon, despite a last-minute injunction request from Premier Doug Ford. The p...

item.title: Mini Crossword and Superquiz for 15 March 2026
item.description: The Age’s 'Superquiz' for Sunday, 15 March 2026 offers readers a selection of puzzles to challenge their minds. Among the featured activities is a Mini Crossword consisting of ten clues and twenty-fiv...

item.title: Kenyan Single Mother of Six Finds Love and Acceptance with German Husband
item.description: Marina, a Kenyan woman, courageously left an abusive marriage while four months pregnant, embarking on the challenging journey of single motherhood. Raising six children alone, she faced depression, f...

item.title: Steve Lopez Discusses the Benefits and Risks of AI in Writing and Education
item.description: Columnist Steve Lopez reflects on the growing presence of artificial intelligence in daily life, particularly its role in writing and communication. Lopez shares his personal experience with AI tools ...

item.title: Tensions Escalate in Gulf After Attacks on UAE and Iranian Oil Facilities
item.description: A major flare-up has erupted in the Gulf region as Iran threatened retaliation following US airstrikes on its main oil hub at Kharg Island. On Saturday, a drone attack disrupted operations at key UAE ...

item.title: Confusion Erupts at Taraba PDP Congress After Two Chairmen Declared
item.description: For Taraba State PDP congress wey happen on Saturday, wahala burst as the congress committee announce two different people as consensus chairman. First, dem announce Victor Samuel Falack and the party...

item.title: Nick Daicos’ positioning in the second half affects Collingwood’s loss to Adelaide
item.description: In the recent AFL clash, Collingwood suffered a pivotal loss to Adelaide, largely due to the absence of Nick Daicos in the centre of play during a critical seven-minute window in the second half. Alth...

item.title: World Urges Stronger Measures to Conserve Forests and Natural Resources
item.description: As we dey approach International Day of Forests on March 21, environmental experts, governments and local community groups dem dey push for stronger action to protect forests and manage natural resour...

item.title: Cignal Super Spikers secure qualifying round berth with win over Capital1
item.description: The Cignal Super Spikers dominated the Capital1 Solar Spikers in a decisive match, securing their spot in the qualifying round of the PVL All-Filipino Conference. The match, which took place on March ...

item.title: Understanding Conversation and the Role of Prediction in Hearing Loss
item.description: Conversation may seem effortless, but it involves remarkable cognitive coordination. Humans typically respond within 200 milliseconds of a speaking turn ending, yet producing a word takes at least 600...

item.title: Kenyan football clubs protest over unpaid funds and warn of possible match boycotts
item.description: Several Kenyan football teams are up in arms over what they describe as persistent delays and shortfalls in payments from the Football Kenya Federation (FKF). They complain that money promised to them...

item.title: President Ruto Joins Kapsabet Boys High School Centenary in Alumni Uniform
item.description: On Thursday, 12th March 2026, President William Ruto made headlines when he attended the centenary celebrations of Kapsabet Boys High School dressed in the school's uniform. The event marked 100 years...

item.title: Salman Ali Agha reprimanded by ICC for on-field misconduct during Bangladesh ODI
item.description: In the second ODI between Pakistan and Bangladesh in Dhaka, Salman Ali Agha received an official reprimand from the International Cricket Council (ICC) for breaching Level 1 of the ICC Code of Conduct...
